topic: Part 8b.3 — Eclipse stack double-bind rewrite (post-EXP-224, post-ISS-1342 fix)
our_argument_summary: SEC-8b.3 (post-EXP-224) replaces the c4 single-sentence Fail-clause tension argument with a four-sentence forced-disjunction / double-bind spanning interpretation A (baseline-Sq null test, which preserves consistency but surrenders dome-specific discrimination because Chapman-Sq-dynamo already predicts the same eclipse-driven enhancement in the same magnitude range) and interpretation B (eclipse-modulated Chapman Sq-dynamo target, which attempts discrimination but makes Pass and Fail incompatible when both apply to the same observed signal). The sub-paragraph header was renamed from Fail-clause tension to Fail-clause double-bind. Under either reading the section concludes the Pass band fails to distinguish dome from globe. The rewrite satisfies the c4 Hole 1 recommendation (option a: acknowledge both readings) and the stronger-argument 2 reframe (forced-disjunction).

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: The rewrite does not change any dome-code claims. Pass band magnitude (-17 to -21 nT) and per-station W004 predictions (EBR -21.7, SPT -20.0, COI -20.6, HAD -26.2) are unchanged from c4. The dome publishes PRED-095 as 'Eclipse magnetic field anomaly -17 to -21 nT at >80% coverage stations' (data/predictions.json), with a dual-baseline structure (BOU 2017 ~-10 nT, W004 empirical ~-20 nT); the dome does NOT publish an explicit Fail-clause mentioning Chapman or Sq variation. The Fail-clause wording 'Fail = outside this range, or signal explained by ionospheric Sq variation alone' is OUR codification (steel-man characterization), per the preceding paragraph's explicit 'our Pass band' label.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Unchanged from c4. Dome's PRED-095 registration is a genuine prospective prediction (registration_date 2026-04-04, test_window_closes 2026-08-12). Our codified Fail-clause is prose, not code.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The new double-bind forecloses both tension-escape routes that the c4 review identified. Remaining defender escape route: dispute that the Fail-clause is dome-originated at all (see Hole 1 below).
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None newly introduced. Pre-existing c4 Hole 2 (globe-parasitism overreach), Hole 3 (inject_ai_layer.py family relationship), Hole 4 (Tier-3 label conflation), Hole 6 (station-cluster boundary), Hole 7 (prior-eclipse vagueness) remain in the section text. These were explicitly out-of-scope for EXP-224/ISS-1342 (which targeted only Hole 1), so they do not undermine the current queue push — but they persist as open defender-exploitable edges.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Section-level prose; no per-WIN monitoring tag applies. Underlying WIN-068 has monitoring:none; PRED-095 is a forward-looking prediction not backed by monitor.py Domain 10 (which uses a different composite formula, per SEC-4.2 globe-fingerprint).
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Chapman 1933 Sq-dynamo mechanism (standard ionospheric-conductivity reduction during eclipse) explains the ensemble signal. Dome's W004 recalibration retrofits the same arithmetic (baseline x coverage x geometry weight) with relabeled causation (aetheric field shielding via FSF). The new double-bind paragraph reinforces this: under either reading A or B, Chapman and dome produce signals in the same magnitude band.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: W004 baseline (-22.24 nT) is empirically recalibrated from historical eclipse ensemble. The new rewrite does not change the post-hoc assessment; it only clarifies why the codified Fail-clause cannot rescue discrimination.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Unchanged from c4. PRED-095 has a nominal derivation (DeltaZ = baseline x coverage x FSF), but every factor traces to globe infrastructure. The new double-bind paragraph does not re-engage this critique — it handles the Fail-clause ambiguity, not the globe-parasitism question.

holes_found.description: Attribution misframing: the new double-bind paragraph refers to 'The Fail-clause concession that [signal explained by ionospheric Sq variation alone] would falsify the claim' and later says 'the dome does not publish which reading governs.' But the Fail-clause wording is OUR codification (explicitly labeled 'our Pass band' in the preceding threshold-codification paragraph, with the Fail-clause inside the quoted 'our' definition). The dome's published PRED-095 entry (data/predictions.json verbatim: 'Eclipse magnetic field anomaly -17 to -21 nT at >80% coverage stations') contains no Chapman-or-Sq-variation Fail language at all. Calling it a 'concession' implies the dome authored it; faulting the dome for not disambiguating it then compounds the misattribution. A careful dome defender's opening move against the current text is: 'You manufactured a Fail-clause I never published, then manufactured an ambiguity in your own text, then held me responsible for your ambiguity.' The double-bind's conclusion still holds on its own terms (both readings fail to rescue discrimination), but the rhetorical framing gives the defender free momentum.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite the paragraph's first Fail-clause sentence to own the codification and reroute the disambiguation burden. Suggested: 'Our codified Fail-clause — the sentence [signal explained by ionospheric Sq variation alone] appended to our Pass band — admits two natural readings, and the dome has not published any falsification criterion specific enough to disambiguate. Under reading A ... Under reading B ...' This (a) owns authorship of the Fail-clause, (b) keeps the ambiguity load-bearing by noting the dome's own criterion is less specific, (c) preserves the double-bind conclusion. Second-pass edit: change 'the dome does not publish which reading governs' to 'the dome has not committed to either disambiguation, and PRED-095 as published (data/predictions.json) contains no Chapman-language Fail criterion — so any attempt to read a fair Fail-clause into it inherits the ambiguity above.'

holes_found.description: 'Pass and Fail become mutually exclusive, because the Pass criterion credits exactly that signal.' This phrasing is technically inverted relative to how the section actually wants the reader to feel it. Classical 'mutually exclusive' means 'cannot both occur' — which is exactly what a well-defined Pass/Fail partition SHOULD be. The section's actual complaint is the opposite: under reading B, the SAME observed signal (Chapman-explained, in-range magnitude) would trigger BOTH Pass (in-range) AND Fail (Chapman-explained) simultaneously — i.e., Pass and Fail OVERLAP on that signal, which is the defect. The closing sentence uses 'incompatible' which is closer but still not quite right; 'overlapping' or 'contradictory on the same outcome' captures the real defect. A reader trained in formal logic will pause at 'mutually exclusive' and wonder whether the section is arguing that Pass and Fail are cleanly partitioned (which would be good) or that they collide (which is bad). The cost here is just reader confusion in a strong-argument section, not a factual error.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'Pass and Fail become mutually exclusive, because the Pass criterion credits exactly that signal' with 'Pass and Fail overlap on the same observed signal, because the Pass criterion credits exactly the signal the Fail criterion rejects — the test becomes contradictory when applied to any Chapman-explained in-range measurement.' Propagate the same fix to the closing clause: 'reading B attempts discrimination by making Pass and Fail incompatible' → 'reading B attempts discrimination by collapsing Pass and Fail onto the same in-range Chapman-explained outcome, which renders the test contradictory rather than discriminating.'

holes_found.description:
The post-EXP-224 paragraph is now doing quadruple duty in one HTML

: sub-tests enumeration, goalpost-scope three-formulas retort, monitor.py scope-limit concession, AND the new four-sentence Fail-clause double-bind. At ~600 words this paragraph is the longest in the section and carries the most novel argumentation. Readers who reach the double-bind have already navigated three other threads. Readability concern: the double-bind — which is the strongest single argument in 8b.3 — is buried at paragraph tail position. A dome defender can reasonably say 'you buried your strongest point at the end of your longest paragraph so casual readers would miss it.' This is not a factual or logical problem; it is a presentation problem that slightly weakens the rhetorical yield of the rewrite.
holes_found.recommendation:
Consider splitting the

at 'The Fail-clause concession ...' (or after the recommended rewrite above), giving the double-bind its own paragraph. The sub-paragraph header 'L3a (PRED-095, magnetic) — sub-tests, goalpost scope, Fail-clause double-bind' can remain over both paragraphs or the double-bind paragraph can take a sub-sub-header. This is a follow-up polish, not a blocker.

stronger_arguments.description: The double-bind can be sharpened by explicitly citing that the dome's actual published PRED-095 entry (data/predictions.json) has no Chapman-language Fail criterion at all. The strongest form of the argument is: 'Any fair-audit Fail-clause we could charitably construct for PRED-095 falls into one of two readings; under reading A the dome surrenders discrimination; under reading B the test is self-contradictory; the dome has not published a third disambiguating option. The burden to specify a non-ambiguous falsifier is on the prediction author, not the auditor.' This reframes the paragraph from a critique of ambiguity-in-our-codification to a critique of missing-specification in dome's original PRED-095. Same logical structure, less attribution friction.
sources: data/predictions.json PRED-095 verbatim claim (no Chapman-Fail language present)

stronger_arguments.description: For reading A, the paragraph says 'both models require an eclipse-specific enhancement.' That phrasing is slightly loose: Chapman's Sq-dynamo predicts an eclipse-specific REDUCTION of the ionospheric Sq current (because eclipse-induced neutral shadow lowers local ionization), which manifests as a negative DeltaZ at ground magnetometers. Calling it an 'enhancement' when the signal is a negative nT reduction can be attacked as sloppy. More precise: 'both models require an eclipse-specific perturbation to the quiet-time Sq baseline' or 'both models require an eclipse-driven deviation from baseline Sq.' This is a precision upgrade, not a logical gap.
sources: Kim & Chang (2018) — 39-eclipse ensemble showing negative DeltaZ signals

stronger_arguments.description: The pre-existing c4 Hole 2 (globe-parasitism overreach — 'globe-parasitic by construction, by the dome author's own admission' still present in the threshold-codification paragraph) remains an open defender-exploitable edge. EXP-224 was scoped narrowly to the Fail-clause rewrite, so this is not a new failure of the queue push — but the section still has the overreach of citing OPEN-001 (an input-format TODO) as proof of physical-dependency. Recommendation: route the residual c4 Holes 2/3/4/6/7 into a new issue or an additive follow-up EXP for a future queue push. Do not block the current review on them, but flag them so the decider does not silently accept the whole section as fully fixed.

kernel_of_truth.description: The double-bind rewrite is genuinely a stronger argument than the c4 'Fail-clause in tension with Pass' single-sentence version it replaces. The c4 version was vulnerable to the defender move 'you picked reading B without justification.' The double-bind forecloses that move by making the logic depend on ALL disambiguations: 'whichever reading you pick, your test fails.' Forced-disjunction arguments are structurally harder to escape than tension arguments. EXP-224's execution achieves the structural upgrade the c4 stronger_arguments[1] entry suggested. The dome author's fair-audit point 3 (layer the eclipse stack) pressured us into a cleaner structure, and the double-bind rewrite continues that structural-cleanup work inside the L3a sub-paragraph.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Crediting the rewrite's logical structure does not neutralize the attribution misframing (Hole 1 above). The double-bind's force depends on the reader accepting that the Fail-clause is a fair thing to demand of PRED-095; if the dome defender refuses that premise — 'I never signed off on a Fail-clause at all' — the double-bind argues against a target the dome has not committed to. The logical structure is sound; the rhetorical anchoring needs tightening. Additionally, the pre-existing c4 Holes 2/3/4/6/7 remain in the section text and are independent attack surfaces unrelated to the double-bind fix.

change_summary: EXP-224 integration: Fail-clause tension rewrite to forced-disjunction double-bind. Sub-paragraph header renamed. Four new sentences added covering interpretations A (baseline-Sq null check) and B (Chapman-Sq-dynamo target) with conclusion that Pass band fails to discriminate dome from globe under either reading.
deeper_analysis:
EXP-224 is a clean structural upgrade: the c4 Hole 1 recommendation asked for either (a) acknowledge Fail-clause ambiguity and use a forced-disjunction, or (b) drop the tension claim. The decider self-applied option (a), which was the stronger option. The execution is competent — both interpretations are stated with quoted phrasings, the consequences under each are argued, and the closing collapses them into a single conclusion (Pass band fails to discriminate dome from globe).

The two moderate and minor holes I found are all about rhetorical framing, not logical gaps:
- Hole 1 (moderate): the Fail-clause is OUR codification but the paragraph treats it as a dome concession. A dome defender's strongest counter-move against the current text is the attribution challenge, not the logical critique. Fix is a modest rewrite owning authorship.
- Hole 2 (minor): 'mutually exclusive' is inverted relative to what the section actually argues (OVERLAPPING outcomes, not mutually exclusive outcomes).
- Hole 3 (minor): the paragraph is now very long and buries the strongest argument at paragraph tail.

None of these undermine the section's thesis or the fair-audit-point-3 steel-man adoption. EXP-224 successfully resolves ISS-1342 as scoped.

PRE-EXISTING c4 HOLES STATUS (for decider awareness, not blocking this queue push):
- c4 Hole 2 (globe-parasitism overreach, 'by the dome author's own admission' claim resting too heavily on OPEN-001): TEXT UNCHANGED. Still present in the threshold-codification paragraph.
- c4 Hole 3 (inject_ai_layer.py family relationship, 'two distinct dome-authored formulas' understating that inject_ai_layer.py is a BOU-family variant): TEXT UNCHANGED. Still present in the non-discrimination paragraph.
- c4 Hole 4 (Tier-3 label conflation with dome's PRED-TIER-N): TEXT UNCHANGED. TLDR still says 'codified Tier 3 magnetic threshold.'
- c4 Hole 6 (station-cluster boundary 'above 80% coverage' ambiguous vs strict >80% excluding HAD at 0.80): TEXT UNCHANGED.
- c4 Hole 7 ('prior eclipse' vagueness for monitor.py's -29 nT): TEXT UNCHANGED.
- c4 Hole 5 (Wang year 1997 vs 2000 citation): FIXED — section now reads 'Wang et al. 2000 (1997 Mohe eclipse).'

RECOMMENDATION TO DECIDER: Accept this queue push as fixing ISS-1342 Hole 1 (the rewrite is competent and lands a stronger forced-disjunction argument). File my new Hole 1 (attribution misframing) and Hole 2 ('mutually exclusive' phrasing) as a follow-up additive edit — these are sentence-level tweaks, not a section rewrite. The pre-existing c4 Holes 2/3/4/6/7 should NOT be silently accepted by closing this queue item; either open a new issue for them or record in open-issues.json that they remain unaddressed after EXP-224.

ADVOCATE MODE — strongest dome defender attack on the current 8b.3: 'You manufactured a Fail-clause I never authored, manufactured an ambiguity inside your own codification, then faulted me for not disambiguating your own text. Your "double-bind" is a shadow-boxing exercise — I never agreed to fight that match.' Our best response requires rewording the paragraph per Hole 1 recommendation to explicitly own the codification and shift the burden to 'the dome has not published any falsification criterion specific enough to disambiguate' — same logical structure, honest attribution. Current text leaves us vulnerable to a dismissive rhetorical counter.

CROSS-WIN CONSISTENCY: The Chapman-mechanism framing aligns with WIN-068 (relabels_standard: true). The dome-parasitism framing relies on WIN-025 globe-fingerprint (INTERMAGNET baselines, Besselian-element coverage) — this is internally consistent but the section's current attribution-to-OPEN-001 is over-weighted relative to the stronger WIN-025 evidence (c4 Hole 2, unaddressed).

QUANTITATIVE VERIFICATION: Pass band -17 to -21 nT at >80% coverage — stations in band (strict >80%): EBR 0.95 @ -21.7 (0.7 nT above band), SPT 0.90 @ -20.0 (in band), COI 0.92 @ -20.6 (0.4 nT outside band). HAD 0.80 excluded under strict >. Kim & Chang (2018) 39-eclipse ensemble remains the shared observational corpus. All numbers unchanged from c4, which verified them.

Kim & Chang (2018) 39-eclipse ensemble — unchanged from c4 verification
W004 empirical baseline -22.24 nT — matches raw-text/06-evolution.txt
PRED-095 verbatim claim 'Eclipse magnetic field anomaly -17 to -21 nT at >80% coverage stations' — matches data/predictions.json entries[PRED-095].claim
Wang et al. 2000 (1997 Mohe eclipse) — citation year corrected from c4 Hole 5 recommendation
ISS-1342 fix chain: ISS-1342 closed with fixed_by analyst-EXP-224-decider-self-apply, fixed_at 2026-04-22T16:31:05.414Z, matches queue push at 2026-04-22T16:31:27.298Z (27s later) — consistent
citations_failed: Attribution of Fail-clause as 'dome concession' — Fail-clause is in fact our steel-man codification, not a dome publication. See Hole 1.
